zh.lifeSummary: 當Anne King於大約1515年出生在英國越可爾吉斯本時，她的父親Richard King27歲，母親Elizabeth Cocke24歲。她於1535年與Thomas Lister III在英國越可爾吉斯本結婚。他們至少是10個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1571年10月3日在她的家鄉享年57歲，埋葬在英國越可爾吉斯本。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Anne King于大约1515年出生在英国越可爾吉斯本时，她的父亲Richard King27岁，母亲Elizabeth Cocke24岁。她于1535年与Thomas Lister III在英国越可爾吉斯本结婚。他们至少是10个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1571年10月3日在她的家乡享年57岁，埋葬在英国越可爾吉斯本。

nameMeanings.name: King
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English king ‘king’ (Old English cyning, cyng), perhaps acquired by someone with kingly qualities or as a pageant name by someone who had acted the part of a king or had been chosen as the master of ceremonies or ‘king’ of an event such as a tournament, festival or folk ritual. In North America, the surname King has absorbed several European cognates and equivalents with the same meaning, for example German König (see Koenig ) and Küng, French Roy , Slovenian, Croatian, or Serbian Kralj , Polish Krol . It is also very common among African Americans. It is also found as an artificial Jewish surname.
English: occasionally from the Middle English personal name King, originally an Old English nickname from the vocabulary word cyning, cyng ‘king’.
Irish: adopted for a variety of names containing the syllable rí (which means ‘king’ in Irish).

variants.name: Kingdon
meanings: English (southwestern, especially Devon): habitational name from Higher Kingdon in Alverdiscott, or from Kendon in North Bovey, both in Devon. Both are named in Old English from cyning ‘king’ (see King ) or cyne- ‘royal’ + dūn ‘hill’.

variants.name: Chief
meanings: Native American: translation into English (and shortening) of a personal name based on a word, such as Cheyenne veho, meaning ‘chief’. See also King 4.

variants.name: Koenig
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Kurt, Otto, Hans, Erwin, Dieter, Frieda, Monika, Reinhard, Egon, Fritz, Heinz, Wilhelm.

German (mainly König) and Jewish (Ashkenazic; also König): from Middle High German künic, German König ‘king’, hence a German nickname for a servant or retainer of a king (for example, a farmer on a royal demesne), or alternatively a status name for the head of a craftmen's guild, or a society of sharpshooters or minstrels. As a Jewish surname, it is artificial, one of several such Ashkenazic names based on European titles of nobility or royalty. The surname Koenig is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. In eastern Germany the surname König is in part a Germanized form (translation into German) of Sorbian Kral ‘king’. Similarly, in Czechia, Poland, Slovenia, and Croatia, it was often applied as a translation into German of Czech, Polish, Slovenian, and Croatian cognates Král (see Kral ), Król (see Krol ), and Kralj , respectively. Compare Konig .

variants.name: Kings
meanings: English (Worcestershire): variant of King , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s. — Note: The population figure published by the US Census Bureau for this surname in the year 2000 is as high as 434, while the figure for 2010 is supposedly lower than 100.

nameMeanings.name: Anne
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

variants.name: Anni
meanings: Pet form of   Ann   or   Anne .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

en.lifeSummary: When Anne King was born about 1515, in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England, her father, Richard King, was 27 and her mother, Elizabeth Cocke, was 24. She married Thomas Lister III in 1535, in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 10 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 3 October 1571, in her hometown, at the age of 57, and was buried in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Anne King est née environ 1515, à Gisburn, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Richard King, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Cocke, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Lister III en 1535, à Gisburn, Everwyck, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 10 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 3 octobre 1571, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans, et a été enterrée à Gisburn, Everwyck, Angleterre.

Sie hat 1535 in Gisburn, Yorkshire, England Thomas Lister III geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 10 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 3. Lei sposato Thomas Lister III nel 1535, nel Gisburn, Yorkshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 10 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 3 ottobre 1571, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 57 anni, e fu sepolta a Gisburn, Yorkshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Anne Kingが約1515年にイギリスヨークシュアイアギスブルンで生まれた時，父Richard Kingは27歳，母Elizabeth Cockeは24歳であった。1535年にイギリスヨークシュアイアギスブルンでThomas Lister IIIと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも10人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1571年10月3日に故郷で57歳で死去し，イギリスヨークシュアイアギスブルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Anne King(이)가 대략 1515년 영국 요르크실르 지스번에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard King(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Cocke(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1535년 영국 요르크실르 지스번에서 Thomas Lister III(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1571년 10월 3일 그녀의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 지스번에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Thomas Lister III em 1535, em Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 10 filhos e 4 filhas. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Anne King родилась около 1515 г. в местности гисбурн, еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Richard King было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Cocke, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Lister III в 1535 г. в местности гисбурн, еркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 10 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла 3 октября 1571 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 57 лет и была похоронена в местности гисбурн, еркшир, Англия.

Se casó con Thomas Lister III en 1535, en Gisburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 10 hijos y 4 hijas.
